channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Cuba suffers another massive power outage leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Cuba has suffered a massive power outage affecting Havana and the country’s provinces that has left millions in the dark.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister as country deals with Trump's trade war
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney was sworn in as Canada’s new prime minister on Friday, and will now try to steer his country through a trade war brought by U.S. President Donald Trump, annexation threats and an expected federal election.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Greenland political parties join together against Trump comments about annexing the island
item.description: LONDON (AP) — All five parties in Greenland’s parliament issued a joint statement on Friday rejecting President Donald Trump’s latest effort to take control of the strategic Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: State Department imposes sanctions on Thai officials after Uyghur men are deported to China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The State Department on Friday announced sanctions on an unknown number of current and former Thai officials for their role in deporting at least 40 Uyghur men to China despite worries that they could be jailed or even face death there.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: West Virginia Republican's bill would ban visas for Chinese students
item.description: Freshman Rep. Riley Moore is backing legislation that would prohibit all Chinese nationals from obtaining student visas, in an effort to prevent them from spying on the American government or stealing advanced technology.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Philippine ex-President Duterte appears by video link at International Criminal Court hearing
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — Jailed former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte appeared Friday by videoconference before judges at the International Criminal Court, days after his arrest in Manila on murder charges linked to the deadly “ war on drugs ” that he oversaw while in office.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Killing site in Jalisco, Mexico just the latest in a long series of gruesome discoveries
item.description: Prosecutors in the western Mexican state of Jalisco have confirmed the discovery of many clothing items and bone fragments by a group of people searching for relatives at a previously known cartel training site.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Whipsawed by Trump's tariffs, the US public is getting a lot more nervous about the economy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's volatile tariff threats are unleashing historic jumps in public anxiety, with the potential to undermine his pledges to strengthen a U.S. economy that is increasingly weakened.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Head of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria has been killed, Iraqi prime minister says
item.description: Iraqi Prime Minister Minister Mohammed Shia al-Sudani has said that the head of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria was killed in an operation by members of the Iraqi national intelligence service along with U.S.
item.dc:creator: Qassim Abdul-Zahra, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American draws Australian PM's ire after snatching wombat
item.description: Australian immigration officials could bar an American influencer from ever returning Down Under for carrying a baby wombat away from its mother.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Allies preserve unity even as Trump's tariffs and Canada taunts overshadow G7 meeting
item.description: Top diplomats from the Group of 7 industrialized democracies are jointly expressing support for Ukraine and a U.S. ceasefire proposal in the three-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas says it will release American hostage, agrees to restart Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: Hamas said Friday that it agreed to release American Israeli hostage Edan Alexander and the bodies of four other Americans, following a proposal from mediators to resume negotiations with Israel over a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy accuses Putin of trying to stall ceasefire talks, pushes Trump for tougher sanctions
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is pushing President Donald Trump to impose sanctions on Russia, as he believes Russian President Vladimir Putin is trying to prolong the war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas agrees to release Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage
item.description: Hamas agreed Friday to release Edan Alexander, the last living American hostage in captivity in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US envoy is taking Putin's comments on Ukraine ceasefire proposal to Trump, Kremlin official says
item.description: Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov says Russian President Vladimir Putin met with U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff to discuss details of the American proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: China, Russia and Iran call for end to US sanctions on Iran and the restart of nuclear talks
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Representatives of China, Russia and Iran called Friday for an end to U.S. sanctions on Iran over its rapidly advancing nuclear program and a restart to multinational talks on the issue. The talks are the latest attempt to broach the matter and come after U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Europe's military personnel shortfalls exposed as Trump warns US security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In the year after Russia launched outright war on Ukraine, NATO leaders approved a set of military plans designed to repel an invasion of Europe. It was the biggest shake-up of the alliance’s defense readiness preparations since the Cold War.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Poll: Majority of Americans think Trump is too close to Russia
item.description: A majority of Americans think President Trump is too closely aligned with Russia, including more than a quarter of Republicans, a poll released Thursday found.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Trump signals cautious optimism on Russia accepting Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump on Thursday said he hoped Russia would “do the right thing” as his administration presses for a ceasefire agreement between Moscow and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Putin says he agrees in principle with US proposal for 30-day ceasefire in Ukraine
item.description: “So the idea itself is correct, and we certainly support it,” Putin told a news conference in Moscow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pakistan's leader meets survivors of deadly train hijacking and the commandos who ended the siege
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistan’s prime minister on Thursday commended the country’s armed forces for successfully rescuing 339 passengers after a deadly train hijacking by insurgents in the southwest. A total of 25 people were killed by the attackers.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland calls on US to place nukes within its borders amid Russia threat
item.description: Polish President Duda has once again called on the US to place its nuclear arms within its borders to better deter Russia, mirroring talks with France amid security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens 200% tariff on European wine after proposed whiskey tariff
item.description: Trump vowed a new escalation in his trade war if the EU pushes ahead with the planned 50% tariff on American whiskey.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Detained captain of cargo ship that collided with US oil tanker is Russian national, ship owner says
item.description: A Russian national was revealed to be the captain of the cargo ship that collided with an American tanker off the coast of England, spilling U.S. military jet fuel into the sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia waiting for US-Ukraine cease-fire proposal details, Kremlin says
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov told reporters Wednesday that Russia must get the details on U.S.-Ukrainian cease-fire talks before commenting on the proposal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: They were forced to scam others worldwide; now thousands are detained on the Burmese border
item.description: Survivors have found themselves in crowded facilities without medical care; 7,000 people were freed last month after being trapped in locked compounds in Burma where they were forced to scam people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctors declare Pope no longer in imminent danger, but remains hospitalized
item.description: Doctors said Monday that Pope Francis is no longer in imminent danger of death as a result of pneumonia but have decided to keep him hospitalized for several more days to receive treatment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope follows Vatican's spiritual retreat from afar as recovery continues
item.description: Pasolini delivered a meditation on “The hope of eternal life,” a theme that was chosen well before Francis was admitted to Rome's Gemelli hospital on Feb. 14 with a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader Sunday as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Panama releases dozens of detained deportees from US into limbo following human rights criticism
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — After weeks of lawsuits and human rights criticism, Panama on Saturday released dozens of migrants who were held for weeks in a remote camp after being deported from the United States, telling them they have 30 days to leave the Central American nation.
item.dc:creator: BY MEGAN JANETSKY, ALMA SOLÍS and MATÍAS DELACROIX, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon released from prison
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday, a day after a Seoul court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial for rebellion without being detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9
